1.Zach DezenzoRHBHOU vs Kendry Rojas (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.227 / xBA 0.241 / xSLG 0.407 (-88 pts)Park -4 pts14d 0.299vs LHP —K 19.8%BB 10.0%HR 3.9%Hit 10.8%Edge Pitcher

Zach Dezenzo RHB vs Kendry Rojas LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 112+480+245, pitcher rows 136+0+0

Verdict

Rojas' 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach Dezenzo should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.227 xwOBA (-88 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -81 pts (bb_type-adj 0.312 -> final 0.227); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kendry Rojas is in a 14-day hot stretch (-48 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

4-Seam Fastball48.50.3110.305GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.311+00.3010.2330.38730.5

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 71.0 mph
swing length 7.2 ft
attack angle 6.2°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.8-0.717-5.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Changeup-7.3+18.76+11.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-7.3+6.2*2-1.0on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

2.Carlos CortesLHBATH vs Walbert Ureña (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.228 / xBA 0.165 / xSLG 0.313 (-87 pts)Park -3 pts14d 0.282vs RHP 0.291K 19.6%BB 11.0%HR 0.3%Hit 14.7%Edge Pitcher

Carlos Cortes LHB vs Walbert Ureña RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 468+375+0, pitcher rows 474+0+0

Verdict

Ureña's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Carlos Cortes should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.228 xwOBA (-87 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -60 pts (raw 0.303 -> adj 0.243); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.243 -> final 0.228); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Carlos Cortes is on a 14-day slump (-99 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Changeup43.80.3070.282GB 63% / LD 5% / FB 29% / PU 4%0.239-680.2070.1310.26438.2

Sweeper9.80.3200.317GB 56% / LD 8% / FB 28% / PU 7%0.268-520.2690.1930.3530.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 68.5 mph
swing length 6.9 ft
attack angle 7.1°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Changeup-7.5+13.828+6.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Sweeper-8.7+11.48+2.7on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

3.Otto KempRHBPHI vs Nick Lodolo (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.228 / xBA 0.224 / xSLG 0.398 (-87 pts)Park +2 pts14d —vs LHP —K 28.1%BB 5.4%HR 1.8%Hit 12.5%Edge Pitcher

Otto Kemp RHB vs Nick Lodolo LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 101+828+0, pitcher rows 165+2497+1865

Verdict

Lodolo's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Otto Kemp should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.228 xwOBA (-87 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -72 pts (bb_type-adj 0.298 -> final 0.228); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Rojas' 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.229 xwOBA (-86 pts vs league, Pitcher). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 5.Chase DeLauterLHBCLE vs Framber Valdez (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.230 / xBA 0.193 / xSLG 0.251 (-85 pts)14d 0.347vs LHP —K 23.1%BB 6.0%HR 0.0%Hit 17.0%Edge Pitcher

Chase DeLauter LHB vs Framber Valdez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 672+27+0, pitcher rows 800+2898+2683

Verdict

Valdez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Chase DeLauter should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.230 xwOBA (-85 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -71 pts (raw 0.301 -> adj 0.230); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Framber Valdez is in a 14-day rough patch (+66 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

6.Joe MackLHBMIA vs JR Ritchie (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.236 / xBA 0.256 / xSLG 0.458 (-79 pts)Park -4 pts14d 0.191vs RHP 0.209K 21.1%BB 15.5%HR 0.0%Hit 13.2%Edge Pitcher

Joe Mack LHB vs JR Ritchie RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 158+0+0, pitcher rows 366+0+0

Verdict

Ritchie's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Joe Mack should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.236 xwOBA (-79 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +27 pts (raw 0.318 -> adj 0.345); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -105 pts (bb_type-adj 0.345 -> final 0.236); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

7.Miguel VargasRHBCWS vs Bryan Woo (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.248 / xBA 0.220 / xSLG 0.347 (-67 pts)Park -20 pts14d 0.525vs RHP 0.496K 23.2%BB 3.8%HR 4.0%Hit 17.3%Edge Pitcher

Miguel Vargas RHB vs Bryan Woo RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 781+2326+1009, pitcher rows 770+2784+1717

Verdict

Woo's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Miguel Vargas should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.248 xwOBA (-67 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -31 pts (raw 0.308 -> adj 0.278); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.278 -> final 0.248); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Miguel Vargas is on a 14-day heater (+117 pts vs season). Bryan Woo is in a 14-day hot stretch (-109 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

8.Jonah HeimLHBATH vs Walbert Ureña (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.250 / xBA 0.179 / xSLG 0.297 (-65 pts)Park -4 pts14d —vs RHP —K 20.8%BB 11.1%HR 2.2%Hit 15.3%Edge Pitcher

Jonah Heim LHB vs Walbert Ureña RHP

Verdict

Ureña's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jonah Heim should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.250 xwOBA (-65 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -40 pts (raw 0.284 -> adj 0.244); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.244 -> final 0.250); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

9.Kyle KarrosRHBCOL vs MacKenzie Gore (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.255 / xBA 0.193 / xSLG 0.338 (-60 pts)14d 0.271vs LHP —K 26.9%BB 9.0%HR 0.0%Hit 19.5%Edge Pitcher

Kyle Karros RHB vs MacKenzie Gore LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 667+637+0, pitcher rows 836+2819+2995

Verdict

Gore's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Karros should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.255 xwOBA (-60 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -58 pts (raw 0.323 -> adj 0.266); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.266 -> final 0.255); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kyle Karros is on a 14-day slump (-60 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

10.Jeremiah JacksonRHBBAL vs Shane McClanahan (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.256 / xBA 0.237 / xSLG 0.404 (-59 pts)Park -18 pts14d 0.270vs LHP —K 28.5%BB 7.1%HR 1.9%Hit 19.9%Edge Pitcher

Jeremiah Jackson RHB vs Shane McClanahan LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 488+707+0, pitcher rows 629+0+0

Verdict

McClanahan's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jeremiah Jackson should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.256 xwOBA (-59 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +5 pts (raw 0.304 -> adj 0.309); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -35 pts (bb_type-adj 0.309 -> final 0.256); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shane McClanahan is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 11.Derek HillRHBCWS vs Bryan Woo (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.262 / xBA 0.213 / xSLG 0.374 (-53 pts)Park -26 pts14d —vs RHP —K 30.2%BB 2.3%HR 2.3%Hit 22.0%Edge Pitcher

Derek Hill RHB vs Bryan Woo RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 216+579+609, pitcher rows 770+2784+1717

Verdict

Woo's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Derek Hill should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.262 xwOBA (-53 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.285 -> adj 0.276); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.276 -> final 0.262); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Bryan Woo is in a 14-day hot stretch (-109 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

12.Chase MeidrothRHBCWS vs Bryan Woo (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.263 / xBA 0.232 / xSLG 0.363 (-52 pts)Park -20 pts14d 0.270vs RHP 0.210K 22.9%BB 3.0%HR 0.9%Hit 24.1%Edge Pitcher

Chase Meidroth RHB vs Bryan Woo RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 768+2057+0, pitcher rows 770+2784+1717

Verdict

Woo's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.263 xwOBA (-52 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.279 -> adj 0.288); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Bryan Woo is in a 14-day hot stretch (-109 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

14.Munetaka MurakamiLHBCWS vs Bryan Woo (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.267 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.394 (-48 pts)Park -23 pts14d 0.383vs RHP 0.413K 25.9%BB 8.4%HR 6.8%Hit 14.2%Slight Pitcher

Munetaka Murakami LHB vs Bryan Woo RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 863+0+0, pitcher rows 770+2784+1717

Verdict

Woo's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Munetaka Murakami should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.267 xwOBA (-48 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -19 pts (raw 0.324 -> adj 0.305); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.305 -> final 0.267); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Bryan Woo is in a 14-day hot stretch (-109 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Taylor Ward RHB vs Shane McClanahan LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 935+2791+2601, pitcher rows 629+0+0

Verdict

McClanahan's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Taylor Ward should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.268 xwOBA (-47 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -41 pts (raw 0.325 -> adj 0.284); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Taylor Ward is on a 14-day slump (-68 pts vs season). Shane McClanahan is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Sung-Mun Song LHB vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 88+0+0, pitcher rows 665+3245+1713

Verdict

Yamamoto's sinker is the projected matchup advantage, while Sung-Mun Song should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.268 xwOBA (-47 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.297 -> adj 0.286); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 66.0 mph
swing length 7.0 ft
attack angle 2.6°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.5+3.711-0.8on plane
Split-Finger-7.2+2.6*0-4.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Curveball-10.1+2.6*2-7.5swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Cutter-5.5+1.17-4.4swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Sinker-4.7+2.6*4-2.1on plane
Slider-7.3+2.6*3-4.7swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

18.Lawrence ButlerLHBATH vs Walbert Ureña (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.273 / xBA 0.178 / xSLG 0.322 (-42 pts)Park -2 pts14d 0.262vs RHP 0.257K 25.7%BB 16.0%HR 1.7%Hit 14.3%Slight Pitcher

Lawrence Butler LHB vs Walbert Ureña RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 618+2521+1816, pitcher rows 474+0+0

Verdict

Ureña's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Lawrence Butler should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.273 xwOBA (-42 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -37 pts (raw 0.294 -> adj 0.257); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.257 -> final 0.273); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 72.3 mph
swing length 7.1 ft
attack angle 5.8°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Changeup-7.5+12.2142+4.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-7.1-1.374-8.4swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
4-Seam Fastball-5.5+0.6298-4.9swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Sweeper-8.7+16.520+7.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

21.Jonny DeLucaRHBTB vs Trevor Rogers (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.276 / xBA 0.235 / xSLG 0.407 (-39 pts)Park -8 pts14d 0.262vs LHP —K 20.8%BB 7.5%HR 2.0%Hit 18.7%Slight Pitcher

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 389+225+1350, pitcher rows 521+1627+2248

Verdict

Rogers' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jonny DeLuca should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.276 xwOBA (-39 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -28 pts (bb_type-adj 0.311 -> final 0.276); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Changeup-8.0+11.529+3.5swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-6.4+2.020-4.4swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Cutter-8.0+0.110-7.9swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Sweeper-8.0+5.9*5-2.0on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

22.Tristan PetersLHBCWS vs Bryan Woo (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.278 / xBA 0.217 / xSLG 0.393 (-37 pts)Park -23 pts14d 0.281vs RHP 0.306K 21.4%BB 6.0%HR 0.6%Hit 23.5%Slight Pitcher

Tristan Peters LHB vs Bryan Woo RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 444+46+0, pitcher rows 770+2784+1717

Verdict

Woo's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Tristan Peters should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.278 xwOBA (-37 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.292 -> final 0.278); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Bryan Woo is in a 14-day hot stretch (-109 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

4-Seam Fastball57.90.2960.298GB 22% / LD 24% / FB 40% / PU 14%0.299+30.2820.1980.39214.2

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

23.Jackson MerrillLHBSD vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.279 / xBA 0.219 / xSLG 0.324 (-36 pts)Park -20 pts14d 0.318vs RHP 0.331K 25.7%BB 5.6%HR 2.8%Hit 20.6%Slight Pitcher

Jackson Merrill LHB vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 741+1748+2291, pitcher rows 665+3245+1713

Verdict

Yamamoto's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Jackson Merrill should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.279 xwOBA (-36 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -38 pts (raw 0.305 -> adj 0.267); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +32 pts (bb_type-adj 0.267 -> final 0.279); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

24.Miguel RojasRHBLAD vs Michael King (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.279 / xBA 0.221 / xSLG 0.374 (-36 pts)Park -9 pts14d —vs RHP —K 20.1%BB 5.9%HR 0.7%Hit 23.3%Slight Pitcher

Miguel Rojas RHB vs Michael King RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 265+1210+1261, pitcher rows 831+1238+2993

Verdict

King's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Miguel Rojas should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.279 xwOBA (-36 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -16 pts (raw 0.304 -> adj 0.288); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sinker36.20.3020.333GB 54% / LD 21% / FB 22% / PU 4%0.308+70.3260.2610.4431.4
Sweeper24.10.3030.291GB 38% / LD 27% / FB 24% / PU 11%0.298-50.2740.2140.35231.8
4-Seam Fastball20.90.3210.311GB 32% / LD 12% / FB 39% / PU 18%0.252-690.2480.1570.31012.0
Slider9.70.3060.319GB 55% / LD 17% / FB 15% / PU 13%0.284-220.2880.2430.36129.6
Changeup9.10.3050.282GB 53% / LD 25% / FB 9% / PU 13%0.299-70.2660.2110.32041.3

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

25.Oswald PerazaRHBLAA vs J.T. Ginn (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.279 / xBA 0.218 / xSLG 0.361 (-36 pts)Park -6 pts14d 0.278vs RHP —K 30.1%BB 4.2%HR 2.5%Hit 22.7%Slight Pitcher

Oswald Peraza RHB vs J.T. Ginn RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 467+1005+40, pitcher rows 668+1580+520

Verdict

Ginn's slider is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.279 xwOBA (-36 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.290 -> adj 0.277); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.277 -> final 0.279); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

26.Mauricio DubónRHBATL vs Max Meyer (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.279 / xBA 0.233 / xSLG 0.371 (-36 pts)Park -16 pts14d 0.283vs RHP 0.328K 15.7%BB 5.5%HR 1.5%Hit 22.7%Slight Pitcher

Mauricio Dubón RHB vs Max Meyer RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 646+1346+1463, pitcher rows 780+1040+968

Verdict

Meyer's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Mauricio Dubón should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.279 xwOBA (-36 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -23 pts (raw 0.314 -> adj 0.291); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Max Meyer is in a 14-day rough patch (+47 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

28.Gavin SheetsLHBSD vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.281 / xBA 0.218 / xSLG 0.341 (-34 pts)Park -21 pts14d 0.462vs RHP 0.471K 24.0%BB 7.8%HR 3.6%Hit 19.0%Slight Pitcher

Gavin Sheets LHB vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 583+2301+2094, pitcher rows 665+3245+1713

Verdict

Yamamoto's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Gavin Sheets should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.281 xwOBA (-34 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.300 -> adj 0.277); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +24 pts (bb_type-adj 0.277 -> final 0.281); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Gavin Sheets is on a 14-day heater (+99 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Rogers' 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Ryan Vilade should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.281 xwOBA (-34 pts vs league, Pitcher). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

30.Ernie ClementRHBTOR vs Ryan Weathers (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.230 / xSLG 0.379 (-33 pts)Park -9 pts14d 0.305vs LHP —K 15.4%BB 4.3%HR 2.1%Hit 22.9%Slight Pitcher

Ernie Clement RHB vs Ryan Weathers LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 559+2087+1450, pitcher rows 732+642+1417

Verdict

Weathers' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Ernie Clement should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -26 pts (raw 0.313 -> adj 0.287); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

31.Weston WilsonRHBBAL vs Shane McClanahan (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.219 / xSLG 0.370 (-33 pts)Park -17 pts14d —vs LHP —K 29.0%BB 8.6%HR 2.4%Hit 20.0%Slight Pitcher

Weston Wilson RHB vs Shane McClanahan LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 146+536+390, pitcher rows 629+0+0

Verdict

McClanahan's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Weston Wilson should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -23 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.288); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.288 -> final 0.282); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shane McClanahan is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Changeup35.10.3040.276GB 59% / LD 20% / FB 16% / PU 4%0.278-260.2390.1910.28556.9

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

32.Blaze AlexanderRHBBAL vs Shane McClanahan (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.225 / xSLG 0.374 (-33 pts)Park -19 pts14d —vs LHP —K 28.9%BB 8.7%HR 1.6%Hit 21.1%Slight Pitcher

Blaze Alexander RHB vs Shane McClanahan LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 375+1044+714, pitcher rows 629+0+0

Verdict

McClanahan's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Blaze Alexander should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -16 pts (raw 0.307 -> adj 0.291); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.291 -> final 0.282); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shane McClanahan is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball42.00.3360.313GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.322-140.3200.2550.3835.1

Curveball7.60.3080.314GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.293-150.2920.2300.3780.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Evan Carter LHB vs Jose Quintana LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 676+826+649, pitcher rows 562+2275+3093

Verdict

Quintana's sinker is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.283 xwOBA (-32 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -27 pts (raw 0.303 -> adj 0.276); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Jose Quintana is in a 14-day hot stretch (-50 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

35.Luisangel AcuñaRHBCWS vs Bryan Woo (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.284 / xBA 0.240 / xSLG 0.421 (-31 pts)Park -14 pts14d —vs RHP —K 24.6%BB 2.6%HR 0.1%Hit 28.3%Slight Pitcher

Luisangel Acuña RHB vs Bryan Woo RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 331+688+148, pitcher rows 770+2784+1717

Verdict

Woo's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.284 xwOBA (-31 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +23 pts (raw 0.285 -> adj 0.308); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.308 -> final 0.284); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Bryan Woo is in a 14-day hot stretch (-109 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

36.Xander BogaertsRHBSD vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.285 / xBA 0.223 / xSLG 0.388 (-30 pts)Park -15 pts14d 0.307vs RHP 0.320K 24.9%BB 6.9%HR 2.7%Hit 21.9%Slight Pitcher

Xander Bogaerts RHB vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 678+2165+1860, pitcher rows 665+3245+1713

Verdict

Yamamoto's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Xander Bogaerts should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.285 xwOBA (-30 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -16 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.295); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.295 -> final 0.285); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Zach Neto RHB vs J.T. Ginn RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 863+2197+2295, pitcher rows 668+1580+520

Verdict

Ginn's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach Neto should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.286 xwOBA (-29 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -27 pts (raw 0.303 -> adj 0.276); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.276 -> final 0.286); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 38.Isaac ParedesRHBHOU vs Kendry Rojas (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.287 / xBA 0.244 / xSLG 0.374 (-28 pts)Park -5 pts14d 0.280vs LHP —K 14.4%BB 11.6%HR 2.8%Hit 18.3%Slight Pitcher

Isaac Paredes RHB vs Kendry Rojas LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 753+1944+2862, pitcher rows 136+0+0

Verdict

Rojas' 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Isaac Paredes should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.287 xwOBA (-28 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.303); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.303 -> final 0.287); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kendry Rojas is in a 14-day hot stretch (-48 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

39.Kevin McGonigleLHBDET vs Slade Cecconi (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.289 / xBA 0.267 / xSLG 0.421 (-26 pts)Park -6 pts14d 0.325vs RHP 0.320K 16.0%BB 10.1%HR 1.0%Hit 20.6%Slight Pitcher

Kevin McGonigle LHB vs Slade Cecconi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 805+0+0, pitcher rows 776+2116+1298

Verdict

Cecconi's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Kevin McGonigle should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.289 xwOBA (-26 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -16 pts (raw 0.343 -> adj 0.328); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -32 pts (bb_type-adj 0.328 -> final 0.289); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kevin McGonigle is on a 14-day slump (-49 pts vs season). Slade Cecconi is in a 14-day hot stretch (-47 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

40.Manny MachadoRHBSD vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.290 / xBA 0.227 / xSLG 0.395 (-25 pts)Park -17 pts14d 0.259vs RHP 0.261K 25.9%BB 5.8%HR 3.7%Hit 21.3%Even

Manny Machado RHB vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 750+2714+2490, pitcher rows 665+3245+1713

Verdict

Yamamoto's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Manny Machado should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.290 xwOBA (-25 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -19 pts (raw 0.316 -> adj 0.297); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.297 -> final 0.290); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Manny Machado is on a 14-day slump (-43 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

41.Andrew BenintendiLHBCWS vs Bryan Woo (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.290 / xBA 0.234 / xSLG 0.419 (-25 pts)Park -28 pts14d 0.411vs RHP 0.396K 19.0%BB 6.5%HR 4.7%Hit 19.8%Even

Andrew Benintendi LHB vs Bryan Woo RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 551+1729+2001, pitcher rows 770+2784+1717

Verdict

Woo's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Andrew Benintendi should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.290 xwOBA (-25 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.311 -> final 0.290); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Andrew Benintendi is on a 14-day heater (+89 pts vs season). Bryan Woo is in a 14-day hot stretch (-109 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

42.Ty FranceRHBSD vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.291 / xBA 0.224 / xSLG 0.385 (-24 pts)Park -17 pts14d —vs RHP —K 24.2%BB 4.8%HR 2.1%Hit 23.1%Even

Ty France RHB vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 275+1890+2030, pitcher rows 665+3245+1713

Verdict

Yamamoto's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Ty France should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.291 xwOBA (-24 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -14 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.296); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.296 -> final 0.291); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

43.Trea TurnerRHBPHI vs Nick Lodolo (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.293 / xBA 0.214 / xSLG 0.380 (-22 pts)Park +1 pts14d 0.265vs LHP —K 20.4%BB 4.9%HR 2.4%Hit 23.3%Even

Trea Turner RHB vs Nick Lodolo LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 770+2484+2122, pitcher rows 165+2497+1865

Verdict

Lodolo's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Trea Turner should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.293 xwOBA (-22 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.307 -> adj 0.285); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.285 -> final 0.293); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. 4-Seam Fastball28.20.3200.285GB 24% / LD 21% / FB 48% / PU 7%0.302-180.2720.1870.40911.6

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 45.Christian VázquezRHBHOU vs Kendry Rojas (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.293 / xBA 0.247 / xSLG 0.397 (-22 pts)Park -9 pts14d 0.211vs LHP —K 17.3%BB 9.4%HR 1.5%Hit 22.4%Even

Christian Vázquez RHB vs Kendry Rojas LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 362+834+1141, pitcher rows 136+0+0

Verdict

Rojas' 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Christian Vázquez should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.293 xwOBA (-22 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.302 -> adj 0.312); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.312 -> final 0.293); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kendry Rojas is in a 14-day hot stretch (-48 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball48.70.3040.305GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.315+110.3050.2400.3809.2

Sinker9.30.3140.316GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.313-10.3140.2440.3966.6

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

46.Austin MartinRHBMIN vs Tatsuya Imai (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.294 / xBA 0.239 / xSLG 0.396 (-21 pts)Park -7 pts14d 0.309vs RHP 0.279K 23.1%BB 8.8%HR 0.1%Hit 18.8%Even

Austin Martin RHB vs Tatsuya Imai RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 584+749+934, pitcher rows 286+0+0

Verdict

Imai's sinker is the projected matchup advantage, while Austin Martin should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.294 xwOBA (-21 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -6 pts (raw 0.317 -> adj 0.311); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.311 -> final 0.294); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Austin Martin is on a 14-day slump (-45 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

47.Myles StrawRHBTOR vs Ryan Weathers (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.295 / xBA 0.229 / xSLG 0.385 (-20 pts)Park -3 pts14d —vs LHP —K 22.6%BB 7.1%HR 0.8%Hit 24.1%Even

Myles Straw RHB vs Ryan Weathers LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 306+1178+14, pitcher rows 732+642+1417

Verdict

Weathers' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Myles Straw should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.295 xwOBA (-20 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -19 pts (raw 0.313 -> adj 0.294); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.294 -> final 0.295); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

49.Brandon ValenzuelaLHBTOR vs Ryan Weathers (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.296 / xBA 0.235 / xSLG 0.383 (-19 pts)Park -9 pts14d 0.386vs LHP —K 29.1%BB 7.4%HR 3.5%Hit 23.2%Even

Brandon Valenzuela LHB vs Ryan Weathers LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 334+0+0, pitcher rows 732+642+1417

Verdict

Weathers' 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Brandon Valenzuela should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.296 xwOBA (-19 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.315 -> adj 0.305); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Brandon Valenzuela is on a 14-day heater (+65 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

50.Lenyn SosaRHBTOR vs Ryan Weathers (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.296 / xBA 0.219 / xSLG 0.378 (-19 pts)Park -2 pts14d —vs LHP —K 23.1%BB 4.0%HR 3.0%Hit 24.1%Even

Lenyn Sosa RHB vs Ryan Weathers LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 345+2056+1384, pitcher rows 732+642+1417

Verdict

Weathers' sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Lenyn Sosa should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.296 xwOBA (-19 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -39 pts (raw 0.321 -> adj 0.282); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.282 -> final 0.296); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

bat speed 71.1 mph

attack angle 10.1°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.8+3.289-1.5on plane
Changeup-7.5+15.651+8.2swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Sinker-5.6+4.422-1.2on plane
Slider-7.1+15.423+8.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).
